tasks.description:
# Task 23: Combat — Victory, Defeat & Rewards

## Objective
Handle combat end states: victory awards XP and loot and returns to dungeon, defeat shows a game-over screen with Load/Quit options.

## Prerequisites
Task 22 must be complete. CombatManager reports `victory` / `defeat` / `fled`.

## Requirements

### Victory Flow
When `combat.result === 'victory'`:
1. Pause for 500 ms
2. Display Victory overlay on canvas (centered 480x300 box):
```
+--------------------------------------+
|             VICTORY!                |
+--------------------------------------+
|  Defeated 3 Goblins                 |
|                                      |
|  XP Awarded: 150  (37 each)         |
|  Gold Looted: 23                    |
|                                      |
|  Items Found:                       |
|   - Healing Potion                  |
|   - Short Sword                     |
+--------------------------------------+
|  Press ENTER to continue            |
+--------------------------------------+
```

3. Award XP: total enemy XP divided evenly among alive party members. Add to `character.xp`.
4. Award gold: roll each enemy's loot.gold; add total to party gold.
5. Award items: roll loot tables (see below). Add items to party shared inventory or first character's inventory.
6. Check level-up notification: if any character has enough XP to level, show "* Roland has enough XP to train! *" (training happens at Guild in Task 11, not here)
7. ENTER returns to DungeonState; encounter event marked triggered.

### Loot Tables (src/renderer/src/data/loot-tables.json)
```json
{
  "humanoid_common": {
    "rolls": "1d2",
    "entries": [
      { "weight": 50, "item": null },
      { "weight": 20, "item": "healing_potion" },
      { "weight": 15, "item": "torch" },
      { "weight": 10, "item": "short_sword" },
      { "weight": 5,  "item": "longsword_plus_1" }
    ]
  },
  "undead_common": {
    "rolls": "1d2",
    "entries": [
      { "weight": 60, "item": null },
      { "weight": 30, "item": "holy_water" },
      { "weight": 10, "item": "scroll_protection" }
    ]
  }
}
```

### LootRoller (src/renderer/src/combat/LootRoller.ts)
```typescript
export class LootRoller {
  rollEncounterLoot(encounter: Encounter, monsters: MonsterInstance[]): {
    gold: number;
    items: Item[];
  };
  rollLootTable(tableId: string): Item | null;
}
```
- `rollEncounterLoot`: gold = sum of each monster's `loot.gold` rolled; items = for each monster with `loot.lootTable`, roll the table according to `rolls`

### Defeat Flow
When `combat.result === 'defeat'`:
1. Pause for 1 s
2. Display Game Over overlay:
```
+--------------------------------------+
|           GAME OVER                 |
+--------------------------------------+
|   The party has fallen.             |
|                                      |
|   "Their bones will rest here       |
|    forever, in the dark."           |
+--------------------------------------+
|   > Load Last Save                  |
|     Return to Main Menu             |
|     Quit                            |
+--------------------------------------+
```
- Arrow keys select; ENTER confirms
- Load Last Save: opens SaveLoadUI with available slots
- Return to Main Menu: discard run, transition to MAIN_MENU
- Quit: closes the window

### Flee Flow
When `combat.result === 'fled'`:
1. Show brief "Fled from battle!" message (1 s)
2. Return to DungeonState
3. Do NOT mark encounter event as triggered (player may re-encounter)
4. Apply minor penalty: 25% chance of trap-style party damage 1d4 (representing fleeing through danger)

### Persistence
- After victory in a fixed encounter event: mark `event.triggered = true` in current TileMap; serialize to save game
- Random encounters: no persistence needed
- Update gameState.gold and characters[].xp/.inventory

### Tests
- `src/renderer/src/combat/__tests__/LootRoller.test.ts`:
  - rollLootTable respects weights (with mocked RNG, returns expected item)
  - rollEncounterLoot sums gold from all monsters
- `src/renderer/src/screens/__tests__/CombatState.results.test.ts`:
  - Victory awards XP evenly among alive members (dead members get 0)
  - Victory adds gold to party
  - Victory adds items to inventory
  - Defeat shows game-over overlay with 3 options
  - Flee returns to dungeon and doesn't mark event triggered

## Done When
- Combat victory awards XP, gold, and items with overlay summary
- Combat defeat shows game-over with Load/Menu/Quit options
- Flee returns to dungeon with optional damage penalty
- Fixed-encounter events mark triggered after victory
- Random encounter rewards work
- Level-up notification shown for eligible characters
- `npm test` passes
- `npm run build` succeeds

tasks.description:
# Task 24: Combat — Integration with Dungeon

## Objective
Wire the full flow: dungeon encounter triggers real combat with actual monster data, victory returns to dungeon with rewards, fixed encounters don't retrigger, and party state persists.

## Prerequisites
Task 23 must be complete. CombatState fully functional with victory/defeat. Task 18 EncounterState routes to COMBAT placeholder.

## Requirements

### EncounterState → CombatState Transition
Update EncounterState (from Task 18):
- "Fight" option now transitions to `COMBAT` state with `CombatInitData`:
```typescript
stateMachine.transitionTo('COMBAT', {
  encounterId: this.encounter.id,
  partySurprise: this.encounter.surprise === 'party',
  enemySurprise: this.encounter.surprise === 'enemy',
});
```

### CombatState.enter (data: CombatInitData)
1. Load encounter from AssetLoader by id
2. For each `monsters[i] = { monsterId, count }`:
   - Parse `count` (e.g., "2d4") and roll how many
   - For each, load monster definition from `monsters.json`
   - Instantiate `MonsterInstance` (roll HP from hitDice, copy stats, set grid position)
3. Place monsters in grid:
   - Front rank (row 1): up to 4 melee monsters
   - Back rank (row 0): up to 4 ranged/caster monsters
   - Fill left-to-right
4. Place party in grid:
   - Front row (row 4): party slots 1-3
   - Back row (row 5): party slots 4-6
5. Create CombatManager with party, enemies, surprise flags
6. Roll initiative

### Combat → Dungeon Return
After victory/defeat overlay dismissed:
- **Victory**: transition back to DUNGEON. Pass results via state machine data: `{ xp: 150, gold: 23, items: [...] }`.
- **Defeat**: transition to MAIN_MENU (game over flow, see Task 23).
- **Fled**: transition back to DUNGEON. No rewards.

DungeonState.enter handles return data:
- Apply XP, gold, items to gameState (CombatState.exit should already have done this — DungeonState just re-renders)
- Display the victory summary in message log: "Defeated 3 Goblins. +150 XP, +23 gold."
- Mark the originating MapEvent (if any) as `triggered = true`

### Fixed Encounter Persistence
When a fixed encounter event triggers combat and is won:
1. `tileMap.markEventTriggered(eventId)`
2. On save, triggered events are serialized as a list of ids in the map's persistence
3. On load, TileMap re-applies triggered state

### Random Encounter Cooldown
After a random encounter (win, lose, flee):
- Set `gameState.randomEncounterCooldown = 5` (steps)
- Decrement per step; encounters cannot fire while > 0
- Prevents encounter chaining after each step

### Party State Bridging
- HP changes during combat persist after exit (party returns to dungeon wounded)
- Status effects with multi-round duration ('poisoned', 'blessed') carry over
- Dead party members stay dead in dungeon (DungeonHUD shows DEAD; raise dead at Temple)
- Spell slots used in combat stay used (rest at Tavern to recover)

### Tests
- `src/__tests__/integration/dungeon-combat-loop.test.ts`:
  - From DungeonState, step on encounter event → EncounterState → choose Fight → CombatState → resolve victory → return to DungeonState
  - Party HP reflects combat damage
  - Gold and XP added to gameState
  - Fixed encounter event marked triggered; re-stepping doesn't refire
- `src/renderer/src/screens/__tests__/CombatState.integration.test.ts`:
  - CombatState.enter with a real encounter id constructs the correct number of monsters
  - "2d4 Goblins" instantiates between 2 and 8 monsters
  - Surprise round skips the surprised side

### Hooks for Save/Load
- gameState now includes (extend save schema):
  - `triggeredMapEvents: { [mapId: string]: string[] }`
  - `randomEncounterCooldown: number`
  - characters[].statusEffects[] (already present)
- Verify save/load round-trip preserves all of the above

## Done When
- Stepping on a fixed encounter event triggers EncounterState → CombatState
- Combat starts with correct monsters from encounters.json
- Victory awards XP, gold, items; returns to dungeon
- Defeated party shows game over
- Fled returns to dungeon
- Fixed encounters don't retrigger after a win
- Random encounters respect cooldown after combat
- HP, status, spell slots persist from combat into dungeon
- `npm test` passes
- `npm run build` succeeds

# Task 25: Phase 4 Integration Test

## Objective
Verify the complete create → explore → fight → loot → return → heal → save → load loop works end-to-end with the actual combat system rendered on canvas.

## Prerequisites
Tasks 20, 21, 22, 23, 24 must ALL be complete.

## Requirements

### Manual Test Flow
1. Launch app → Main Menu → New Game → create 4-character party: Fighter, Mage, Cleric, Thief
2. Begin Adventure → Town Hub
3. Shop: buy 4 Healing Potions, equip Long Sword + Chain Mail on Fighter
4. Save game to Slot 1
5. Depart → enter Dungeon
6. Move around until a random encounter fires (or step on a fixed encounter event)
7. Encounter screen → Fight
8. **Verify combat screen**:
   - Battlefield with colored monster sprites on canvas
   - Party sprites in front/back rows
   - Round counter and turn indicator at top
   - Pulsing cyan border on active combatant
   - Action menu visible
   - Initiative bar shows turn order
9. Take a turn as the Fighter:
   - Press A (Attack) → reticle appears on a goblin
   - Press ENTER → yellow line animation → red splash → damage number floats up
   - Combat log: "Roland hits Goblin for 7 damage"
10. Enemy turn: 1 s delay → enemy attacks a party member → red splash on party
11. Repeat until victory
12. **Verify victory overlay**:
    - XP awarded
    - Gold looted
    - Items found
    - "Press ENTER to continue"
13. Return to dungeon → message log shows the rewards summary
14. Verify HP bars in party panel reflect combat damage
15. Use a Healing Potion (interact menu or rest at Tavern)
16. Walk back to Town Hub
17. Temple → heal remaining wounds (cost: gp)
18. Tavern → Rest → full heal + advance day
19. Guild → check Fighter's XP → if enough, train to Level 2 → HP and combat stats increase
20. Save game to Slot 2
21. Restart app → Load Slot 2 → verify all state restored:
    - Party at Town Hub
    - Correct gold, XP, levels
    - Inventory and equipment intact
    - Map state, fog of war, triggered events all preserved

### Defeat Test
22. Save to Slot 3 (full HP)
23. Engage a strong encounter (e.g., go deeper into dungeon)
24. Lose the fight intentionally (let HP go to 0)
25. **Verify game over**:
    - Game over overlay appears
    - Three options: Load Last Save / Main Menu / Quit
26. Choose Load Last Save → Slot 3 → verify party restored

### Automated Integration Tests
Create `src/__tests__/integration/full-combat-loop.test.ts`:
```typescript
// Test the full create → explore → fight → return cycle
// 1. Create GameState with 4 characters
// 2. Load test map, step to an encounter event
// 3. Dispatch encounter → instantiate CombatState
// 4. Programmatically resolve combat: party attacks in sequence
// 5. Verify victory: XP/gold/items added to gameState
// 6. Return to dungeon: party HP reflects combat damage
// 7. Save state, restore: all values preserved
```

Create `src/__tests__/integration/level-up.test.ts`:
```typescript
// 1. Create character with 0 XP
// 2. Award enough XP to level up (e.g., 1000 XP for Fighter)
// 3. Train at guild → level = 2, HP increased by hit die + CON
// 4. Spell slots recomputed for casters
```

### Visual Verification Checklist
Update `docs/PHASE-4-VERIFICATION.md`:
```markdown
# Phase 4 Visual Verification

Combat MUST render on canvas (NOT DOM):
- [ ] Battlefield grid with colored combatant sprites
- [ ] HP bars above each combatant
- [ ] Pulsing cyan border on active combatant
- [ ] Initiative strip showing turn order
- [ ] Action menu at bottom
- [ ] Attack animation: yellow line + red splash
- [ ] Damage numbers float upward and fade
- [ ] "MISS" / "CRIT!" text overlays
- [ ] Status effect dots on sprites
- [ ] Victory overlay with rewards summary
- [ ] Game over overlay with three options

If any of these are missing or rendered as DOM text, the phase is INCOMPLETE.
```

### Bug Fixes
Common issues to check:
- Party HP doesn't reset between combats (should persist into dungeon)
- XP awarded to dead members (should be 0)
- Inventory items not added after victory
- Save/Load loses combat-resulting changes if save happens mid-victory-overlay
- Status effects carrying over indefinitely (durations should decrement)
- Memory leak: CombatState not cleaning up animations on exit
- Encounter event refiring after victory

### Cleanup
- Remove all combat-debug console.log statements
- Verify CombatState.exit clears all listeners + timers
- Confirm consistent use of EGA palette in all combat rendering

## Done When
- Manual test flow (steps 1-26) completes without errors
- Combat is fully functional with visual feedback on canvas
- Defeat flow shows game over and load works
- Automated integration tests pass
- Visual verification checklist 100% checked
- `npm test` passes
- `npm run build` succeeds
- `docs/PHASE-4-VERIFICATION.md` committed with all boxes checked
- No console errors during full session

tasks.description:
# Task 26: Spell Memorization System

## Objective
Implement Vancian spell memorization: spellcasters memorize specific spells into level-based slots during rest. The character sheet shows memorized spells and remaining slots.

## Prerequisites
Task 25 must be complete. Spell data exists in `src/renderer/src/data/spells.json`. Class spellcasting fields are in `classes.json`.

## Requirements

### Data Structures (src/shared/types.ts — add)
```typescript
export interface MemorizedSpell {
  spellId: string;
  expended: boolean;
}

export interface SpellSlot {
  level: number;          // 1, 2, 3
  spells: MemorizedSpell[]; // capacity controlled by class.spellcasting.slotsPerLevel
}

// Add to Character interface:
//   spellbook?: string[];      // known spells (mage learns by leveling or scrolls)
//   memorizedSpells?: SpellSlot[];  // slots by level
```

For each class:
- **Mage**: prepares from `spellbook`. Starts with mage L1 spells: Magic Missile, Sleep, Read Magic.
- **Cleric**: knows all cleric spells of accessible levels (divine — no spellbook). Memorizes any cleric spell up to slot count.
- **Paladin**: knows only paladin spells (subset of cleric L1). Memorizes from those.
- **Fighter, Thief, Ranger**: no spellcasting (ranger may gain later levels — out of MVP scope).

### Slots Per Level (from classes.json `spellcasting.slotsPerLevel`)
Confirm data exists; example for Mage:
```
Lv1: { 1: 1 }
Lv2: { 1: 2 }
Lv3: { 1: 2, 2: 1 }
Lv5: { 1: 3, 2: 2, 3: 1 }
```

### SpellMemorizationSystem (src/renderer/src/systems/SpellMemorizationSystem.ts)
```typescript
export class SpellMemorizationSystem {
  // Get capacity at each spell level for a character
  getSlotCapacity(character: Character): Record<number, number>;

  // Get currently memorized spells per level
  getMemorized(character: Character): SpellSlot[];

  // Memorize a spell into a slot of the given level. Returns false if no slot available.
  memorize(character: Character, spellId: string, slotLevel: number): boolean;

  // Forget (clear) a memorized spell at index
  forget(character: Character, slotLevel: number, slotIndex: number): boolean;

  // Mark a memorized spell as expended (used in combat)
  expend(character: Character, spellId: string, slotLevel: number): boolean;

  // Full reset (rest): all expended flags cleared, slots remain memorized
  resetSlots(character: Character): void;

  // Get list of spells a character can memorize at a given level
  getAvailableSpells(character: Character, slotLevel: number): Spell[];
}
```

### Memorization Screen (src/renderer/src/screens/SpellMemorizationScreen.ts)
Accessible from the Tavern "Rest" flow OR from the Guild "View Sheet → Memorize Spells" action.

#### Layout (canvas)
```
+--------------------------------------+
|  MEMORIZE SPELLS — Aelindra (Mage)  |
+--------------------------------------+
| Level 1 Slots: [Magic Missile] [---] |  <- 2 slots, 1 filled
| Level 2 Slots: [---]                 |  <- 1 slot, empty
|                                      |
| Available Spells (L1):              |
|  > Magic Missile (1d4+1 force)      |
|    Sleep (area, sleep effect)       |
|    Read Magic (utility)             |
+--------------------------------------+
| (Enter) Memorize  (X) Forget  (ESC) |
+--------------------------------------+
```

- Top: per-slot-level row showing memorized spells with brackets
- Empty slots shown as `[---]`
- Below: list of available spells for the currently selected slot level
- Player presses 1/2/3 to switch slot level
- Enter on a spell memorizes it into next empty slot of that level
- X on a memorized spell forgets it

### Tavern Integration
When player rests at Tavern (Task 13):
- For each spellcaster in party, call `system.resetSlots(character)` after gold deduction
- Display message: "Aelindra's spells refresh."

### Character Sheet Update
On the character detail view (Task 11, Guild), add a Spells section:
```
+-- Spells --------------------+
| L1: [Magic Missile] [Sleep] |
|     2/2 slots remaining     |
| L2: [---]                   |
|     0/1 slots remaining     |
+-----------------------------+
```
Expended spells shown grayed out.

### New Mage Spellbook Acquisition
- On level up at Guild: if class is mage, automatically learn one spell from the next-available level (player picks from a list)
- Scrolls (item subtype `scroll`): using a scroll out of combat lets a mage attempt to add the spell to spellbook (95% success; on failure, scroll consumed without effect)

### Tests
- `src/renderer/src/systems/__tests__/SpellMemorizationSystem.test.ts`:
  - getSlotCapacity returns class's slot config for the character's level
  - memorize fills empty slot; refuses if capacity full
  - forget clears the slot
  - expend marks as used; can be marked again only after reset
  - resetSlots clears all expended flags
  - getAvailableSpells filters by class + level
- `src/renderer/src/screens/__tests__/SpellMemorizationScreen.test.ts`:
  - Switching slot level changes available spell list
  - Enter on a spell adds to memorized
  - X on memorized spell removes it

## Done When
- Spellcasters can open the memorization screen
- Spells can be memorized into slots up to capacity
- Forget removes a memorized spell
- Character sheet shows memorized spells with slot counts
- Resting resets expended flags (slots become available again)
- Mages learn new spells on level up
- `npm test` passes
- `npm run build` succeeds

tasks.description:
# Task 27: Spell Casting in Combat

## Objective
Wire memorized spells into combat: spellcasters can cast their prepared spells, damage/heal/effect targets, and expend slots. Visual feedback for each spell category.

## Prerequisites
Task 26 must be complete. CombatManager exists with stubbed cast action.

## Requirements

### Spell Resolution
On combat action `{ type: 'cast', spellId, targetIds }`:
1. Validate caster has the spell memorized and not expended
2. Validate spell can be cast in combat (`spell.combatOnly !== false` and `castingTime === 'action'`)
3. Mark spell as expended via SpellMemorizationSystem
4. Apply spell effect to each target
5. Add log entry: "Aelindra casts Magic Missile on Goblin for 4 damage!"

### SpellResolver (src/renderer/src/combat/SpellResolver.ts)
```typescript
export class SpellResolver {
  resolve(caster: Combatant, spell: Spell, targets: Combatant[], combat: CombatManager): SpellResult;
}

export interface SpellResult {
  targetsAffected: Array<{ combatantId: string; damage?: number; healed?: number; statusApplied?: string }>;
  log: string[];
}
```

#### Spell Effect Categories
- **damage**: roll `spell.damage` dice. Each target rolls save if `saveType` set:
  - Save success → half damage
  - Save failure → full damage
- **damage_multi (magic missile)**: roll `spell.damage` for each missile; missile count from `missilesAtLevel[casterLevel]`. Auto-hit unless target has shield/brooch.
- **healing**: roll `spell.healing` dice. Heal each target up to maxHp.
- **status (sleep, paralyze, charm, fear)**: apply `spell.statusEffect` as StatusEffect with `durationRounds` from spell.duration.
- **buff (bless, shield, haste)**: apply positive StatusEffect to allies.
- **utility (read magic, detect magic, light)**: out-of-combat only or trivial in-combat effect.

### Spell Target Selection
Combat target selection extends to spells:
- `spell.targetType`:
  - `single`: pick one enemy or ally
  - `multi`: pick up to N targets
  - `area`: auto-targets all enemies (or allies) in an area; show circular reticle
  - `self`: caster only, no selection needed
  - `all_allies` / `all_enemies`: auto-target
- For area spells, the reticle is a colored radius indicator centered on the chosen tile

### Cast Action UI
When player picks "C" (Cast) from action menu:
1. Open Spell Picker overlay (canvas-drawn box):
```
+----------------------+
| AELINDRA'S SPELLS    |
+----------------------+
| L1: 2/2 slots        |
|  > Magic Missile     |
|    Sleep (used)      |  <-- expended grayed out
+----------------------+
| L2: 0/1 slots        |
|  (no spells memorized)|
+----------------------+
```
2. Player selects a spell → enters target selection
3. ESC backs out to action menu

### Visual Effects per School
- **Evocation (damage)**: yellow projectile or burst from caster to target
- **Healing (cleric)**: green glow spreads over target for 600 ms
- **Enchantment (sleep, charm)**: purple swirl on target
- **Conjuration**: blue summon flash
- **Necromancy**: black/red shadow
- **Divination/Utility**: white shimmer

Each effect renders for ~800 ms before turn ends.

### Save Throws
Use `formulas.ts` `rollSave(target, saveType, dc)`:
```typescript
export function rollSave(target: Combatant, saveType: 'str'|'dex'|'con'|'int'|'wis'|'cha', dc: number): { roll: number; total: number; success: boolean };
```
- d20 + relevant stat modifier + character class/race bonuses (if any)
- Save DC = 10 + caster level + spell-level + caster's primary stat mod (e.g., INT mod for mage)

### Specific Spell Implementations
At minimum, implement these to support MVP:
- **Magic Missile** (mage L1): 1d4+1 force, auto-hit, missiles scale with caster level
- **Sleep** (mage L1): area, puts up to 5d8 HP worth of enemies to sleep (lowest HP first). Sleeping = skip turn; takes damage wakes them.
- **Shield** (mage L1): +5 AC self for 1 round
- **Cure Light Wounds** (cleric L1): heal 1d8+wisMod single target
- **Bless** (cleric L1): +1 to attack rolls for allies, 5 rounds
- **Hold Person** (cleric L2): single target, save vs WIS, paralyzed 1d6 rounds

### Tests
- `src/renderer/src/combat/__tests__/SpellResolver.test.ts`:
  - Magic Missile auto-hits and deals damage
  - Sleep affects targets by lowest HP first up to roll's total
  - Cure Light Wounds heals up to max HP
  - Bless adds +1 attack bonus status to all party
  - Hold Person paralyzes on failed save, no effect on success
  - Save DC scales with caster level and primary stat
- `src/renderer/src/combat/__tests__/SpellCastFlow.test.ts`:
  - Casting expends the slot
  - Cannot cast a spell whose slot is expended
  - Cannot cast a spell not memorized

## Done When
- Spellcasters can cast memorized spells in combat
- Damage spells deal damage with save-for-half where applicable
- Healing spells restore HP
- Status spells apply effects with duration tracking
- Buff spells add positive effects to allies
- Visual effects per school
- Spell slots properly expend (and only reset on rest)
- `npm test` passes
- `npm run build` succeeds

tasks.description:
# Task 28: Inventory & Equipment Polish

## Objective
Add a full inventory management screen: view all items, equip/unequip across all slots, drop items, transfer between characters. Equipment changes are immediately reflected in stats.

## Prerequisites
Task 25 must be complete. ShopState (Task 12) already supports basic buy/sell/equip.

## Requirements

### InventoryScreen (src/renderer/src/screens/InventoryScreen.ts)
Implements `GameStateHandler`. Canvas-rendered. Accessible from:
- Guild → View Sheet → "Manage Inventory"
- Dungeon → ESC menu → "Inventory"

### Layout (two-panel + slot panel)
```
+--------------+------------------+--------------+
| PARTY        | INVENTORY        | EQUIPMENT    |
| > Roland     | > Long Sword     | Wpn: Long Sw |
|   Aelindra   |   Healing Pot.x2 | Arm: Chain M |
|   Durgin     |   Torch x 3      | Shl: —       |
|   Pip        |   Lockpick       | Rng: —       |
|              |                  | Amu: —       |
+--------------+------------------+--------------+
| Item Details:                                  |
|  Long Sword — Weapon                           |
|  Damage: 1d8 slashing                          |
|  Value: 15 gp                                  |
|  "A versatile blade favored by warriors."      |
+------------------------------------------------+
| (E)quip  (U)nequip  (D)rop  (T)ransfer  (ESC) |
+------------------------------------------------+
```

- Left: party list — select active character with up/down or 1-6 keys
- Middle: that character's inventory (scrollable, 12 items per page)
- Right: equipment slots — shows what's worn
- Bottom: item details for the selected item; actions

### Inventory Data Model
Each character has:
```typescript
interface Inventory {
  items: InventoryItem[];        // max 20 items per character (MVP)
  maxSize: 20;
}

interface InventoryItem {
  itemId: string;
  quantity: number;              // for stackables (potions, torches)
}
```

### Actions
- **E (Equip)**: equip selected item to its slot. If slot already occupied, move that item back to inventory.
- **U (Unequip)**: unequip selected slot's item back to inventory. Stats recompute.
- **D (Drop)**: confirmation prompt → remove from inventory. Lost permanently.
- **T (Transfer)**: prompt for destination character → move item (or 1 of a stack). Both characters' inventories must have capacity.
- **TAB**: switch between Inventory list and Equipment panel
- **ESC**: return to previous state

### Stat Recalculation (src/renderer/src/systems/StatCalculator.ts)
Centralize equipment-driven stat calculation:
```typescript
export class StatCalculator {
  recalculateCharacter(character: Character): void;
  // Recomputes:
  //   - AC = 10 + dexMod + (armor.acBonus || 0) + (shield ? 2 : 0) + magic ring/amulet bonuses
  //   - Damage bonus from weapon properties
  //   - Saving throw bonuses from rings/amulets
  //   - Status resistance from items
}
```

Call this:
- After equip/unequip
- After level up
- After spell effect that modifies stats

### Stackable Items
- Healing Potion, Torch, Arrow, Lockpick: stackable
- Stack quantity shown as `x N` after name
- Using one decrements quantity; quantity 0 removes from inventory
- Capacity counts each stack as 1 item

### Class Restrictions (Enforce in Equip)
- Use `item.usableBy` array. If character class not in list → log "Aelindra cannot use Chain Mail" and refuse equip.
- Mages: no armor (except robes), no metal weapons (daggers, staves OK)
- Clerics: no edged weapons (no swords, only blunt — maces, hammers)
- Thieves: light armor only (leather)
- Paladins, Fighters, Rangers: all weapons + armor

### Magical Item Identification (MVP-light)
- Items with `properties.magical === true` have "*" suffix in name when identified
- Unidentified items show as "?? Long Sword ??"
- Identify Spell (cleric L1) reveals magical properties (out-of-scope; mark as future)

### Encumbrance (MVP-light)
- Display total weight: `currentWeight / (strength * 5)` lb
- Over-encumbered: penalty to DEX-based actions (info only; no enforcement in MVP)

### Item Description / Hover
- Selected item's full description in bottom panel
- Includes: type, damage/AC, value, usable-by classes, special properties

### Tests
- `src/renderer/src/screens/__tests__/InventoryScreen.test.ts`:
  - Equipping armor increases AC; unequipping reverts
  - Class restriction prevents wrong-class equip
  - Drop removes item; confirmation required
  - Transfer moves item between characters
  - Inventory capacity enforced
- `src/renderer/src/systems/__tests__/StatCalculator.test.ts`:
  - AC = 10 + dexMod + armor.acBonus + shield + bonuses
  - Damage bonus calculated from weapon
  - Recalc runs after equip event

## Done When
- Inventory screen shows all items per character
- All 5 equipment slots can be equipped/unequipped
- Class restrictions enforced
- Stat changes immediate (AC, damage) and persist
- Transfer between characters works
- Drop with confirmation
- Stackables show quantity
- Item details visible
- `npm test` passes
- `npm run build` succeeds

# Task 29: Phase 5 Integration Test

## Objective
Verify the spell memorization → casting → inventory → equipment loop end-to-end with full visual feedback.

## Prerequisites
Tasks 26, 27, 28 must ALL be complete.

## Requirements

### Manual Test Flow
1. Launch app → New Game → create 4 characters: Fighter, Mage, Cleric, Thief
2. Begin Adventure → Town Hub
3. Shop: buy 2 Healing Potions, equip starter weapons/armor
4. Guild → View Aelindra (Mage) → Manage Spells → Memorize Spells screen
5. **Verify**: 1 L1 slot available, mage spellbook lists 3 spells
6. Memorize Magic Missile → slot fills, screen updates
7. ESC → View Durgin (Cleric) → Memorize Spells
8. Memorize Cure Light Wounds in L1 slot
9. Guild → View Roland → Manage Inventory → see all items
10. **Verify**: Long Sword in weapon slot, Chain Mail in armor slot, AC = 15
11. Unequip Chain Mail → AC drops to 10
12. Re-equip Chain Mail → AC = 15
13. Try equipping Chain Mail on Aelindra → "Aelindra cannot use Chain Mail"
14. Transfer Healing Potion from Roland to Pip → both inventories update
15. Drop Torch → confirm → torch removed
16. ESC → return to Town Hub
17. Depart → enter Dungeon
18. Find a random encounter → Fight
19. On Aelindra's turn: press C (Cast)
20. **Verify**: Spell Picker shows Magic Missile (memorized, not expended)
21. Select Magic Missile → target a goblin → yellow projectile animation
22. **Verify**: Damage dealt, slot now expended (shown grayed out)
23. Try to cast Magic Missile again → cannot select (no slots remaining)
24. On Durgin's turn: press C → Cast Cure Light Wounds → target Roland (injured)
25. **Verify**: Green glow on Roland, HP restored
26. Defeat the encounter
27. Return to dungeon → walk back to Town Hub
28. Tavern → Rest at Inn (cost gold)
29. **Verify**: Aelindra's Magic Missile no longer expended; Durgin's Cure Light Wounds available again
30. Train Aelindra to Level 2 at Guild (if enough XP)
31. **Verify**: Level 2 grants 2 L1 slots (was 1)
32. Memorize Magic Missile and Sleep in 2 slots
33. Save to Slot 1 → Load Slot 1 → all memorized spells, inventory state preserved

### Automated Integration Tests

Create `src/__tests__/integration/spell-loop.test.ts`:
```typescript
// 1. Create Mage character at level 1
// 2. Memorize Magic Missile in L1 slot
// 3. Enter combat, cast Magic Missile → slot expended
// 4. Try to cast again → returns false (no slots)
// 5. Reset slots (simulating rest)
// 6. Cast Magic Missile again → succeeds
```

Create `src/__tests__/integration/inventory-equipment.test.ts`:
```typescript
// 1. Create Fighter with default equipment
// 2. Unequip Long Sword → weapon slot empty, no damage bonus
// 3. Equip a different weapon → damage bonus updates
// 4. Equip Chain Mail → AC increases
// 5. Class restriction: try mage equipping chain mail → fails
// 6. Transfer item between characters
// 7. Save/Load → all inventory state preserved
```

Create `src/__tests__/integration/level-up-spells.test.ts`:
```typescript
// 1. Mage at level 1 with 1 L1 slot
// 2. Award XP → train to level 3 → 2 L1 slots + 1 L2 slot
// 3. Learn new spell (Sleep) on level up
// 4. Memorize Sleep in L1 → expend → reset
```

### Visual Verification Checklist
Update `docs/PHASE-5-VERIFICATION.md`:
```markdown
# Phase 5 Visual Verification

- [ ] Memorization screen shows slot capacity per spell level
- [ ] Available spells filtered by class and level
- [ ] Expended spells visually grayed out
- [ ] Spell Picker overlay opens in combat
- [ ] Visual effects per school:
   - [ ] Yellow projectile for Magic Missile
   - [ ] Green glow for healing
   - [ ] Purple swirl for sleep/charm
- [ ] Damage/heal numbers float on targets
- [ ] Inventory screen shows all slots + items
- [ ] AC/damage recompute after equip/unequip
- [ ] Class restrictions enforced with clear error
```

### Bug Fixes
Common issues:
- Spell slots not persisting through save/load
- Memorized spell still expended after rest (reset bug)
- Equipment recalc not running after unequip
- Class restriction enforced for buy but not equip (or vice versa)
- Stackable items not stacking correctly when transferred
- Visual effect from previous spell still rendering on next turn

### Cleanup
- Remove all Phase 5 debug console.log
- Ensure SpellResolver, StatCalculator, InventoryScreen have no `any` types

## Done When
- Manual test flow (steps 1-33) completes without errors
- Automated integration tests all pass
- Visual checklist 100% checked
- `npm test` passes
- `npm run build` succeeds
- `docs/PHASE-5-VERIFICATION.md` committed with all boxes checked
- No console errors during full session

# Task 30: Overland Map — Canvas Renderer & Travel

## Objective
Build an overland map screen accessible from the Town Gate. Player navigates a regional map with the town, three dungeon entrances, and roads connecting them. Random encounters can occur during travel.

## Prerequisites
Task 25 must be complete. Town Hub routes Depart → OVERLAND state.

## Requirements

### OverlandState (src/renderer/src/screens/OverlandState.ts)
Implements `GameStateHandler`. Canvas-rendered.

### Overland Map (src/renderer/src/data/overland.json)
```json
{
  "id": "overland",
  "name": "The Frontier",
  "width": 40,
  "height": 25,
  "tiles": [
    "............................GGGGGGGGG.",
    "...GGGGGG.....RR....MMMMMMM.G.......G.",
    "..G.....G....R..R..M.......M.G.....G..",
    "..G.[T].G...R....R.M..[D2].M.G.[D3].G.",
    "..G.....G...R....R.M.......M.G.....G..",
    "...GGGGGG..R......RMMMMMMMMMM.GGGGGGG.",
    "..........R........R..................",
    ".........R..........RR................",
    "........R.............R...............",
    ".......R...............R..............",
    "......R................R..............",
    "...FFFR.................R.............",
    "..FFFRRR...........FFFFFRFFF..........",
    ".FFFRRRR..........FF......R...........",
    "FFFRRR..........FF.........R..........",
    "FFRRR..........FF.[D1].....R..........",
    "FFRR...........FFF.........R..........",
    "FFR.............FFFFFFFFFFFR..........",
    "...........................R..........",
    "..........................WWWWW.......",
    ".........................WWWWWWW......",
    "..........................WWWWW.......",
    "........................SSSSSSSSS.....",
    ".......................SSSSSSSSSS.....",
    ".........................SSSSSS......."
  ],
  "locations": [
    { "id": "town",       "name": "Ironhaven",         "x": 5,  "y": 3,  "type": "town", "targetState": "TOWN_HUB" },
    { "id": "warrens",    "name": "Goblin Warrens",    "x": 18, "y": 15, "type": "dungeon", "mapId": "warrens_1f" },
    { "id": "crypt",      "name": "Crypt of Shadows",  "x": 22, "y": 3,  "type": "dungeon", "mapId": "crypt_1f" },
    { "id": "ashenmire",  "name": "Ruins of Ashenmire","x": 35, "y": 3,  "type": "dungeon", "mapId": "ashenmire_1f" }
  ],
  "randomEncounters": { "table": "overland_wilderness", "chance": 0.03 },
  "startPosition": { "x": 5, "y": 4 }
}
```

Tile chars: `.` plains, `G` grass, `R` road, `M` mountains (impassable), `F` forest (slow), `W` water (impassable), `S` swamp (slow + chance of trap), `[T]/[D1]/[D2]/[D3]` location markers (walk on to enter)

### Tile Colors (EGA)
- Plains `.`: dark green #00AA00
- Grass `G`: green #55FF55
- Road `R`: brown #AA5500
- Mountain `M`: light gray #AAAAAA
- Forest `F`: dark green #00AA00 (darker variant)
- Water `W`: dark blue #0000AA
- Swamp `S`: dark brown / olive (use #555555 or interp)
- Location markers: yellow #FFFF55 with town/dungeon glyph

### Rendering
- 640x400 canvas, tile size 16px → fits 40x25 perfectly
- Render entire map (no scroll needed for 40x25)
- Player marker: cyan 14x14 square with facing arrow
- Locations: distinct colored icon (yellow circle + label below)
- Compass top-right (reuse from DungeonHUD)
- Info bar bottom: party gold, day, current biome

### Movement
- Arrow keys move one tile
- Cooldown 200 ms
- Step into mountain/water → "Impassable terrain."
- Step into forest/swamp → slow (extra 100 ms cooldown — flavor)
- Step on location marker → prompt "Enter Ironhaven? (Y/N)" → transition to that state

### Random Encounters During Travel
- Each successful step: roll `random() < 0.03` → encounter
- Use encounter table `overland_wilderness`:
```json
{
  "id": "overland_wilderness",
  "entries": [
    { "encounterId": "enc_wild_wolves", "weight": 30 },
    { "encounterId": "enc_bandits", "weight": 25 },
    { "encounterId": "enc_giant_rats", "weight": 20 },
    { "encounterId": "enc_goblin_scouts", "weight": 25 }
  ]
}
```
- Biome bias: roads → fewer encounters; forests → wolves; swamps → traps too

### Save/Load
- gameState.currentOverlandX, .currentOverlandY
- Day advances by 1 per N steps (e.g., every 10 steps)
- Resting only available at town or via Camp action (TBD if time)

### Camp Action (optional)
- Press C → "Make Camp? Risks ambush. (Y/N)"
- Restores HP (50%) + tries spell rest (no slot reset, but flavor)
- 25% chance of ambush encounter
- Advances day by 1

### Tests
- `src/renderer/src/screens/__tests__/OverlandState.test.ts`:
  - Loading overland map parses tile chars correctly
  - Player cannot walk into water or mountain
  - Walking onto a location triggers transition prompt
  - Random encounter fires based on chance
  - "Enter Ironhaven" transitions to TOWN_HUB
  - Saving preserves overland position

## Done When
- Town "Depart" → OVERLAND with canvas-rendered regional map
- 4 locations: Town, Warrens, Crypt, Ashenmire — all reachable via roads
- Player marker moves with arrows, terrain enforced
- Stepping onto a location prompts to enter
- Random encounters fire during travel
- Saving/loading preserves position
- `npm test` passes
- `npm run build` succeeds

# Task 31: Dungeon Zone 1 — Goblin Warrens (2 Floors)

## Objective
Build the first complete dungeon zone: two floors of the Goblin Warrens with encounters, fixed events, treasure, a boss fight (Hobgoblin Warchief), and stairs connecting the floors.

## Prerequisites
Tasks 24, 28, 30 must be complete. Combat works; inventory works; overland routes here.

## Requirements

### Map Files
Create two JSON map files. Each is 32x32 tiles.

**`src/renderer/src/data/maps/warrens_1f.json`** — Upper floor (entrance from overland)
- Entrance at top-center (16, 1)
- Corridor system with 4 rooms
- 1 locked door requiring "rusty_key" or thief unlock
- 1 secret door hiding a small treasure room
- Stairs down at bottom-right (28, 28) to warrens_2f
- 3 fixed encounter events (goblin patrols)
- 2 treasure events (small loot)
- 1 trap event (1d6 spike trap)
- Random encounter table: `goblin_warrens_1`

Tile layout idea:
```
W W W W W W W W W W W W W W W W . W W W W W W W W W W W W W W W
W . . . . . W . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . . W
W . . . . . D . . . . . . . W W W W W W . . . . . . . . . . . W
...
```
(Generate a coherent maze layout. Use width=32, height=32. Place walls, corridors, rooms.)

Events to include:
```json

  { "id": "ev_warrens1_enc3",    "x": 26, "y": 25, "type": "encounter", "data": { "encounterId": "enc_goblin_patrol" } }
]
```

**`src/renderer/src/data/maps/warrens_2f.json`** — Lower floor (boss floor)
- Stairs up at top-right (28, 1) back to warrens_1f
- Large central chamber with the boss
- 4 fixed encounter events (more goblins + hobgoblin guards)
- 2 treasure events with better loot
- Boss room contains a final encounter `enc_warchief_boss`
- After boss defeated: trigger event reveals a Quest item (Warchief's Banner) — used in quest system (Task 34)
- Random encounter table: `goblin_warrens_2`

Events include:
```json

  { "id": "ev_warrens2_enc1",    "x": 5,  "y": 5,  "type": "encounter", "data": { "encounterId": "enc_hobgoblin_guards" } },

]
```

```json
    { "name": "Battle Cry", "damage": "0", "type": "buff", "effect": "ally_attack_bonus" }
  ],
  "speed": 6,
  "specialAbilities": [
    { "id": "leadership", "name": "Leadership", "description": "All goblinoid allies gain +1 attack while Warchief is alive.", "trigger": "passive" }
  ],
  "xpValue": 300,
  "loot": { "gold": "5d10", "lootTable": "warchief_loot" },
```

### Loot Table (append to loot-tables.json)
```json

  "rolls": "1d2+1",
  "entries": [
    { "weight": 30, "item": "longsword_plus_1" },
    { "weight": 25, "item": "chain_mail_plus_1" },
    { "weight": 25, "item": "healing_potion" },
    { "weight": 20, "item": "warchief_banner" }
  ]
}
```

### Wiring
- Update `warrens_1f.stairsDownTo = "warrens_2f"`, `warrens_2f.stairsUpTo = "warrens_1f"`
- OverlandState location `warrens` maps to `warrens_1f`
- "Enter Goblin Warrens?" prompt loads warrens_1f
- After defeating Warchief: gameState.flags['warchief_defeated'] = true (used by quest system)

### Tests
- `src/renderer/src/screens/__tests__/Warrens.test.ts`:
  - warrens_1f loads, has 32x32 dimensions, correct events
  - Stairs down transitions to warrens_2f
  - Stairs up back to warrens_1f
  - Boss encounter present with hobgoblin_warchief monster
  - Boss defeat sets the flag

## Done When

- Stairs work between floors
- Locked door requires key or thief unlock
- Secret door discoverable
- Boss fight is challenging and drops banner
- Returning to overland after boss preserves "warchief_defeated" flag

# Task 32: Dungeon Zone 2 — Crypt of Shadows (3 Floors)

## Objective
Build the second dungeon zone: three floors of the Crypt of Shadows with undead encounters, the Vampire Lord boss, and environmental hazards (pits, traps, cursed tiles).

Task 31 must be complete.

# Task 33: Dungeon Zone 3 — Ruins of Ashenmire (2 Floors)

## Objective
Build the third dungeon zone: two floors of swamp ruins with environmental hazards (acid pools, swamp gas), serpentine/aberration encounters, and the Black Dragon boss.

## Prerequisites
Task 32 must be complete.

## Requirements

### Map Files
Two 32x32 JSON map files at `src/renderer/src/data/maps/`:

**`ashenmire_1f.json`** — Outer ruins
- Entrance at (16, 1)
- Overgrown ruined courtyards connected by corridors
- 4 fixed encounters (serpents, lizardfolk, swamp ghouls)
- 2 treasure events
- Acid pool tiles (use `TileType.Water` with `data.acid = true` in events): 2d4 damage per step
- 1 puzzle: 4 pressure plates that must be activated in correct order to open a sealed door
- Stairs down to ashenmire_2f at (28, 28)
- Random encounter table: `ashenmire_1`

**`ashenmire_2f.json`** — Dragon's lair
- Stairs up at (28, 1)
- Vast underground lair with central pool
- 3 fixed encounters (drake spawn, lizardfolk shamans)
- 2 treasure events (one is a fake dragon hoard pile)
- Black Dragon boss at center (16, 20)
- After boss: trigger event sets `dragon_defeated` flag and drops "Dragon's Heart" (quest item)
- Lair has acid pool tiles around the boss
- Random encounter table: `ashenmire_2`

### Environmental Hazards
- **Acid Pools**: tiles where event is `{ type: 'trap', data: { damage: '2d4', damageType: 'acid', repeat: true, saveType: 'dex', saveDC: 12 } }`. Step on: party takes acid damage (save for half).

- **Pressure Plate Puzzle**:

  - Activate plates in order 2, 4, 1, 3 to open sealed door to the inner sanctum

### Knockdown / Grapple
- Add new status effects to StatusEffect schema:

  - `grappled` (until released or escape): cannot move
- Snake's Constrict: grapples target until save (STR vs DC 14)
- Dragon's Tail Sweep: knockdown (save DEX vs DC 16)

### Dragon Breath Cooldown

- Monster AI prioritizes breath when multiple party members are clustered

  - Pressure plate puzzle: correct order opens door, wrong order triggers trap

# Task 35: Phase 6 Integration Test

## Objective
Verify the complete game is playable start to finish: all three zones, all quests, combat, spells, inventory, and progression.

## Prerequisites
Tasks 30, 31, 32, 33, 34 must ALL be complete.

## Requirements

### Full Game Playthrough Test
Run the game and complete the entire game:
1. Launch → New Game → create party (1 Fighter, 1 Mage, 1 Cleric, 1 Thief recommended)
2. Begin Adventure → Town Hub
3. Town Hall → talk to Mayor → accept "Hobgoblin Warchief's Banner" quest
4. Shop: stock up on healing potions, equip starter gear
5. Tavern: rest, listen for rumors
6. Save to Slot 1
7. Depart → Overland Map
8. Travel to Goblin Warrens (south)
9. Explore Warrens 1F:
   - Find rusty key (or thief unlock)
   - Trigger 2+ encounters
   - Find treasure
   - Descend stairs
10. Explore Warrens 2F:
    - Defeat hobgoblin guards
    - Find longsword +1 treasure
    - Defeat Hobgoblin Warchief
    - Pick up Warchief's Banner
11. Return to overland → Town Hub
12. Temple → heal up
13. Tavern → rest, advance day
14. Town Hall → turn in quest → receive 500 gp + 1000 XP
15. Guild → train any leveled characters
16. Temple → talk to High Priest → accept "Curse of the Crypt"
17. Overland → travel to Crypt of Shadows (north-central)
18. Explore Crypt 1F → 2F → 3F:
    - Find Holy Symbol of Pelor in 3F
    - Equip on Cleric
    - Defeat Vampire Lord
    - Pick up Crypt Key
19. Return → Temple → turn in quest → receive 1000 gp + 2000 XP + Holy Avenger
20. Equip Holy Avenger on Paladin (or Fighter if no paladin)
21. Town Hall → accept "The Black Dragon's Demise"
22. Overland → travel to Ruins of Ashenmire (east)
23. Explore Ashenmire 1F:
    - Solve pressure plate puzzle (sequence 2-4-1-3)
    - Defeat lizardfolk and snakes
    - Descend stairs
24. Explore Ashenmire 2F:
    - Defeat drake spawn
    - Engage Black Dragon
    - Defeat dragon (manage breath cooldown, use radiant/acid resistance)
    - Pick up Dragon's Heart
25. Return → Town Hall → turn in → receive 5000 gp + 5000 XP + Dragon Slayer Sword
26. **Game complete** — final state save in Slot 5

### Save/Load Stress Test
At various points:
- Save in dungeon mid-exploration → quit → load → verify state intact
- Save mid-quest progression → quit → load → quest state preserved
- Save after combat → load → party HP, XP, status all preserved

### Automated Integration Tests
Create `src/__tests__/integration/full-game.test.ts`:
```typescript
// Test the complete game loop programmatically:
// 1. Create GameState with party
// 2. Accept q_warchief
// 3. Simulate Warrens exploration → Warchief defeat
// 4. Turn in q_warchief → rewards added
// 5. Accept q_crypt (now available)
// 6. Simulate Crypt exploration → Vampire Lord defeat
// 7. Turn in q_crypt → rewards added
// 8. Accept q_dragon
// 9. Simulate Ashenmire exploration → Black Dragon defeat
// 10. Turn in q_dragon → final rewards
// 11. Verify all 3 quests in 'turned_in' state
// 12. Verify party survives with reasonable stats
// 13. Save/load round-trip preserves complete state
```

Create `src/__tests__/integration/quest-prerequisites.test.ts`:
```typescript
// Verify prerequisite enforcement
// 1. Try to accept q_dragon before q_warchief → fails
// 2. Complete q_warchief → q_crypt becomes available, q_dragon still locked
// 3. Complete q_crypt → q_dragon becomes available
```

### Visual Verification Checklist
Create `docs/PHASE-6-VERIFICATION.md`:
```markdown
# Phase 6 Visual Verification

Full game must demonstrate:
- [ ] Overland map renders 4 locations connected by terrain
- [ ] All 3 dungeons playable (Warrens 2F, Crypt 3F, Ashenmire 2F)
- [ ] All boss fights work with their special mechanics:
   - [ ] Warchief: Leadership buff to goblinoids
   - [ ] Vampire Lord: Regeneration, vulnerability to radiant
   - [ ] Black Dragon: Breath weapon cooldown, frightful presence
- [ ] Quest log opens with J, shows all states
- [ ] NPCs at Town Hall and Temple offer quests
- [ ] Quest stages advance with objectives
- [ ] Turn in awards rewards and marks turned_in
- [ ] Prerequisites enforced
- [ ] Endgame: party finishes all 3 quests
```

### Balance Verification
The game should be:
- Beatable with a balanced 4-character party
- Challenging but fair (no soft-locks, no impossible fights at appropriate level)
- ~6-10 hours of content end-to-end

If imbalanced:
- Adjust monster HP, XP awards, or starting equipment
- Document changes in `docs/BALANCE-NOTES.md`

### Bug Fixes
Common issues to hunt:
- Quest objective not advancing despite flag set
- Item objective looks at wrong inventory (party-wide vs character-specific)
- Boss flags not setting after victory
- Prerequisite check using wrong status (active vs turned_in)
- Save/Load losing quest state
- Overland encounters not respecting cooldown after combat
- Door/secret door state lost across save/load
- Triggered events firing again after load

### Cleanup
- Remove all debug logs
- Ensure no `any` types in QuestSystem, OverlandState
- Verify all maps load via AssetLoader with correct ids
- Document remaining known issues in `docs/KNOWN-ISSUES.md`

## Done When
- Full game playable start to finish (steps 1-26 complete)
- All 3 main quests completable in correct order
- Game can be saved/loaded at any non-combat point
- All automated integration tests pass
- Visual verification checklist 100% checked
- `npm test` passes
- `npm run build` succeeds
- `docs/PHASE-6-VERIFICATION.md` committed with all boxes checked
- `docs/BALANCE-NOTES.md` updated if any balance changes made

tasks.description:
# Task 36: Audio System

## Objective
Add an audio system: background music per state, sound effects for combat / exploration / UI, and volume controls. Web Audio API based.

## Prerequisites
Task 35 must be complete. Game is fully playable.

## Requirements

### AudioManager (src/renderer/src/engine/AudioManager.ts)
```typescript
export class AudioManager {
  private musicAudio: HTMLAudioElement | null = null;
  private sfxBuffers: Map<string, AudioBuffer> = new Map();
  private audioContext: AudioContext;
  
  private musicVolume = 0.5;
  private sfxVolume = 0.7;
  private masterVolume = 1.0;

  constructor();
  async init(): Promise<void>;
  
  // Music — loops a background track
  async playMusic(trackId: string, fadeMs?: number): Promise<void>;
  stopMusic(fadeMs?: number): void;
  
  // SFX — fire-and-forget one-shot
  async loadSfx(id: string, url: string): Promise<void>;
  playSfx(id: string): void;
  
  setMusicVolume(v: number): void;       // 0..1
  setSfxVolume(v: number): void;
  setMasterVolume(v: number): void;
  getMusicVolume(): number;
  getSfxVolume(): number;
  getMasterVolume(): number;
}
```

### Audio Assets (src/renderer/src/assets/audio/)
For MVP, ship procedural placeholder audio (no licensing concerns) or attribution-free assets. If using procedural:
- Use AudioContext oscillators + envelopes to generate retro-style chiptune music and SFX
- Each track is a short loopable pattern (8-16 seconds)

Required tracks:
- **music/main_menu.ogg** — soft melodic theme
- **music/town.ogg** — peaceful tavern-like loop
- **music/dungeon.ogg** — ambient dark loop with sparse percussion
- **music/combat.ogg** — energetic battle theme
- **music/boss.ogg** — dramatic orchestral-style boss theme
- **music/overland.ogg** — adventurous travel theme
- **music/victory.ogg** — short fanfare (non-loop)
- **music/defeat.ogg** — somber stinger (non-loop)

Required SFX:
- **sfx/menu_select.ogg** — short blip
- **sfx/menu_confirm.ogg** — affirmative click
- **sfx/footstep.ogg** — dungeon step
- **sfx/door_open.ogg** — wooden creak
- **sfx/door_locked.ogg** — rattle
- **sfx/sword_hit.ogg** — metallic clang
- **sfx/sword_miss.ogg** — whoosh
- **sfx/spell_cast.ogg** — magical hum
- **sfx/heal.ogg** — gentle chime
- **sfx/damage.ogg** — generic hit
- **sfx/enemy_die.ogg** — defeat sound
- **sfx/treasure.ogg** — pickup chime
- **sfx/trap.ogg** — sharp warning
- **sfx/level_up.ogg** — victory chord
- **sfx/save.ogg** — confirmation tone
- **sfx/typewriter.ogg** — text scrolling effect (per character or chunk)

### State Music Mapping
Hook into GameEngine state changes — when entering a state, call AudioManager.playMusic:
```typescript
const MUSIC_BY_STATE: Record<string, string> = {
  MAIN_MENU: 'main_menu',
  CHARACTER_CREATION: 'main_menu',
  TOWN_HUB: 'town',
  TOWN_HALL: 'town',
  TEMPLE: 'town',
  SHOP: 'town',
  TAVERN: 'town',
  GUILD: 'town',
  OVERLAND: 'overland',
  DUNGEON: 'dungeon',
  COMBAT: 'combat',
  ENCOUNTER: 'combat',
  GAME_OVER: 'defeat',
};
```

Special cases:
- Boss encounters: detect `encounter.isBoss` and play `boss.ogg` instead of `combat.ogg`
- Victory overlay: play `victory.ogg` once (no loop)
- Defeat overlay: play `defeat.ogg` once

### SFX Trigger Points
- MainMenu navigation: menu_select
- MainMenu confirm: menu_confirm
- Dungeon step: footstep
- Wall bonk: damage (small)
- Door open: door_open
- Door locked: door_locked
- Combat attack hit: sword_hit
- Combat attack miss: sword_miss
- Spell cast: spell_cast
- Healing applied: heal
- Combatant death: enemy_die
- Trap triggered: trap
- Treasure found: treasure
- Level up at Guild: level_up
- Save game: save

### Settings Screen (src/renderer/src/screens/SettingsScreen.ts)
Canvas-rendered. Accessible from Main Menu and ESC menu in any state.

```
+--------------------------------------+
|             SETTINGS                |
+--------------------------------------+
| Master Volume:  [████████░░]  80%   |
| Music Volume:   [██████░░░░]  60%   |
| SFX Volume:     [███████░░░]  70%   |
|                                      |
| Music: [On]                         |
| SFX:   [On]                         |
|                                      |
| > Save Settings                     |
|   Reset to Defaults                 |
|   Back                              |
+--------------------------------------+
```

- Left/Right adjusts volume in 10% steps
- Persist to localStorage as `fd2_settings`
- Load on app start

### Tests
- `src/renderer/src/engine/__tests__/AudioManager.test.ts`:
  - playMusic sets currentTrack
  - stopMusic clears currentTrack
  - playSfx triggers audio buffer source (mocked)
  - Volume setters clamp to 0..1
  - Settings persist to localStorage and restore on init

## Done When
- Each game state plays appropriate background music
- All SFX triggers fire at the right moments
- Boss encounters play boss music
- Victory/defeat play one-shot stingers
- Settings screen lets player adjust volumes and toggle mute
- Settings persist across app sessions
- No audio glitches or stuck loops
- `npm test` passes
- `npm run build` succeeds

tasks.name: Visual Polish & Screen Transitions
tasks.doneWhen: Smooth transitions, no visual glitches, consistent EGA aesthetic, 60fps.
tasks.description:
# Task 37: Visual Polish & Screen Transitions

## Objective
Polish the visual experience: smooth state transitions, consistent EGA aesthetic, no visual glitches, and stable 60fps.

## Prerequisites
Task 35 must be complete. Audio (Task 36) preferred but parallel-safe.

## Requirements

### Screen Transitions (src/renderer/src/rendering/TransitionEffect.ts)
```typescript
export type TransitionType = 'fade' | 'wipe_left' | 'wipe_right' | 'pixelate' | 'circle';

export class TransitionEffect {
  start(type: TransitionType, durationMs: number, onMidpoint?: () => void): void;
  render(ctx: CanvasRenderingContext2D): void;
  isActive(): boolean;
}
```

Apply transitions on state changes:
- Main Menu → Character Creation: fade (300 ms)
- Character Creation → Town: fade (500 ms)
- Town → Overland: wipe_left (400 ms)
- Overland → Dungeon: pixelate (500 ms)
- Dungeon → Combat: circle expand from party position (400 ms)
- Combat → Dungeon: circle contract (400 ms)
- Any state → Game Over: fade to red (1000 ms)

Implementation hook in GameEngine.transitionTo():
```typescript
this.transition.start('fade', 300, () => {
  this.stateMachine.transitionTo(newState, data);
});
```

### Visual Consistency Audit

#### EGA Palette Enforcement
Run an audit script that scans all rendering code for hex colors:
```bash
grep -rE "#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}" src/renderer/src --include="*.ts" | grep -v 'EGA_PALETTE'
```
Any color not in EGA_PALETTE should be replaced with the closest match.

Create `src/shared/EGAPaletteValidator.ts`:
```typescript
export function isEGAColor(color: string): boolean;
export function nearestEGAColor(color: string): string;
```

Wrap CanvasRenderingContext2D in `EGARenderingContext` (dev-only) that warns if non-EGA color used.

#### Font Consistency
- Use monospace font 'Courier New' consistently
- Font sizes: 8px (small UI), 10px (body), 12px (highlights), 16px (titles), 24px (screen titles)
- Define in `src/shared/constants.ts`:
```typescript
export const FONT_FAMILY = 'Courier New, monospace';
export const FONT_SIZE = {
  small: 8,
  body: 10,
  highlight: 12,
  title: 16,
  big: 24,
};
```

#### Letterboxing on Resize
- Canvas always renders at 640x400 internally
- CanvasRenderer scales to fit window with nearest-neighbor, maintains 16:10 aspect
- Black bars on the sides if window is wider; bars top/bottom if taller
- No stretching or blurring

### Frame Rate
- Target: 60 fps
- GameEngine uses `requestAnimationFrame` and tracks `dt`
- Add FPS counter overlay (toggle with F3) — yellow text top-left:
```
FPS: 60
DT: 16ms
```
- Profile and optimize any state rendering above 16 ms

Common optimizations:
- Pre-render static tile colors to off-screen canvases
- Cache HP bar / minimap into off-screen buffers when not changing
- Batch fillRect calls where possible
- Avoid allocating arrays/objects in render loop

### Animation Polish
- Camera scroll easing when party moves (smooth lerp over 100 ms, not instant)
- HP bar drain animation: red flash when damage taken, bar drains over 300 ms
- Combat damage numbers: cubic easing for upward float, alpha fade-out
- Menu cursor: subtle pulsing (alpha 0.7 → 1.0 over 1s, sine wave)
- Title screen: subtle particle effect (falling pixels or sparkles in EGA colors)

### Particle System (src/renderer/src/rendering/ParticleSystem.ts)
```typescript
export interface Particle {
  x: number; y: number;
  vx: number; vy: number;
  life: number;     // 0..1 fraction remaining
  color: string;
  size: number;
}

export class ParticleSystem {
  spawn(x: number, y: number, opts: Partial<Particle>): void;
  spawnBurst(x: number, y: number, count: number, opts: Partial<Particle>): void;
  update(dt: number): void;
  render(ctx: CanvasRenderingContext2D): void;
  clear(): void;
}
```

Use cases:
- Combat damage splashes (red, 8 particles)
- Heal sparkles (green, 12 particles, rising)
- Spell cast glow (school-colored, around caster)
- Title screen ambient (slow falling pixels)
- Treasure pickup (yellow sparkles)

### Pixel Snap & Anti-aliasing
- Ensure `ctx.imageSmoothingEnabled = false` for crisp pixels
- Set CSS `image-rendering: pixelated` on canvas
- Round all draw positions to integers to avoid sub-pixel blurring

### Glitch Fixes
- DungeonState: ensure stale tiles cleared between renders (no ghost trails)
- CombatState: clean up old animations on combat end
- StateMachine: previous state's DOM (if any) fully removed before new state's render
- No flicker when state transitions
- No "first frame" artifacts on state enter

### Accessibility (light pass)
- High-contrast EGA colors already give good accessibility baseline
- Add subtitle/caption option in Settings (Task 36 setup): if enabled, show on-screen text for audio cues
- Add "colorblind hint mode": status effects show icon + letter (not just color)

### Tests
- `src/renderer/src/rendering/__tests__/TransitionEffect.test.ts`:
  - fade transition completes in the specified duration
  - isActive() returns true during transition, false after
  - onMidpoint callback fires at 50% through duration
- `src/renderer/src/rendering/__tests__/ParticleSystem.test.ts`:
  - spawnBurst creates the requested number of particles
  - update decrements life by dt; expired particles removed
  - render draws each particle at its current position with current alpha
- `src/shared/__tests__/EGAPaletteValidator.test.ts`:
  - isEGAColor accepts all 16 palette colors
  - isEGAColor rejects arbitrary colors like '#FF0000'
  - nearestEGAColor maps off-palette colors to closest match

## Done When
- All screen transitions play smoothly with appropriate effects
- No visual glitches, ghost trails, or flickers between states
- 60 fps maintained throughout typical play (FPS counter via F3)
- All colors are EGA palette (validator confirms)
- Pixel-perfect rendering with no blurring
- Particle effects play for combat splashes, heals, treasure, casting
- Settings include subtitle and colorblind-hint toggles
- Letterboxing works on any window aspect ratio
- `npm test` passes
- `npm run build` succeeds

tasks.description:
# Task 38: Final Integration & Packaging

## Objective
Package the game for distribution: `npm run build` produces a working Electron app, the game is playable start to finish with no critical bugs, and the final deliverable is ready to ship.

## Prerequisites
Tasks 35, 36, 37 must ALL be complete.

## Requirements

### Final Build Configuration

#### `package.json` — add packaging
```json
"scripts": {
  "dev": "electron-vite dev",
  "build": "electron-vite build",
  "test": "vitest run",
  "preview": "electron-vite preview",
  "package": "npm run build && electron-builder",
  "package:win": "npm run build && electron-builder --win",
  "package:mac": "npm run build && electron-builder --mac",
  "package:linux": "npm run build && electron-builder --linux"
},
"build": {
  "appId": "com.fleet.forgotten-depths-ii",
  "productName": "Forgotten Depths II",
  "directories": {
    "output": "dist-electron"
  },
  "files": [
    "out/**/*",
    "package.json"
  ],
  "win": { "target": "nsis" },
  "mac": { "target": "dmg" },
  "linux": { "target": "AppImage" }
},
"devDependencies": {
  "electron-builder": "^24.0.0"
}
```

Install electron-builder:
```bash
npm install --save-dev electron-builder
```

#### Icon Assets
Create `build/` directory with:
- `icon.png` (512x512 EGA-style icon — dungeon door with sword)
- `icon.ico` (Windows)
- `icon.icns` (macOS)

The icon should use EGA colors and depict the game's theme.

### Final Smoke Test
Run an end-to-end smoke test from packaged build:
1. `npm run package` → produces installer in `dist-electron/`
2. Install on a clean machine (or fresh VM)
3. Launch from desktop shortcut
4. Complete the entire game (steps from Task 35 manual flow)
5. Verify no crashes, no missing assets, no broken transitions
6. Verify saves persist across app restarts (saved to userData/saves)

### About Screen Update
Update the About screen (from Task 05) with final version info:
```
FORGOTTEN DEPTHS II
A Gold Box Tribute RPG

Version 1.0.0

Created by AI Fleet Dev Team
Project Lead: Virgil (AI Fleet Manager)
Stakeholder: Jon Wazar

Game mechanics derived from the D&D 5e SRD 5.1
© 2016 Wizards of the Coast, Inc.
Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License

Built with Electron + TypeScript + HTML5 Canvas
```

### README.md
Update `README.md` with:
- Game description
- System requirements (OS, RAM, disk)
- Installation instructions
- Controls reference
- Screenshots (canvas captures of: main menu, town, dungeon, combat, victory)
- Build from source instructions
- License (MIT for code, SRD 5.1 CC-BY 4.0 for game mechanics content)
- Credits

### CONTROLS.md
Create a controls reference at the repo root:
```markdown
# Controls

## Universal
- ESC: Open menu / Go back
- F3: Toggle FPS counter
- M: Toggle minimap (in dungeon)

## Menus
- Arrow keys: Navigate
- Enter/Space: Select
- Mouse: Click to select

## Dungeon
- Arrow keys / WASD: Move
- Q/E: Rotate facing
- Space: Interact (doors, stairs)
- J: Open quest log
- I: Open inventory (when wired)

## Combat
- A: Attack
- C: Cast spell
- D: Defend
- U: Use item
- F: Flee
- Arrow keys: Select target
- Enter: Confirm
```

### Final QA Pass

#### Critical Bug Hunt
Run through this checklist; fix any issues found:
- [ ] No app crashes during full playthrough
- [ ] No missing music/SFX (every state has audio)
- [ ] No broken state transitions (no black screens of death)
- [ ] All quests completable
- [ ] All save slots work
- [ ] Save/Load works at every non-combat point
- [ ] No memory leaks (use Chrome DevTools heap snapshots in dev mode)
- [ ] Window resize doesn't break rendering
- [ ] Closing window during combat saves no corrupted state
- [ ] Replaying after victory works (start new game from main menu)

#### Performance
- [ ] 60 fps in all states on target hardware
- [ ] Startup time < 3 seconds
- [ ] Save file size < 100 KB
- [ ] Memory usage stable (no growth over time)
- [ ] Package size reasonable (< 200 MB)

#### TypeScript Cleanup
- [ ] Zero TypeScript errors (`tsc --noEmit` clean)
- [ ] No `any` types in critical paths
- [ ] All public APIs have JSDoc comments
- [ ] No unused imports/variables

### Known Issues
Finalize `docs/KNOWN-ISSUES.md` with any remaining quirks or post-1.0 backlog items:
- Out-of-scope features (multiclass at level up, gnome class restrictions, etc.)
- Future enhancements (more dungeons, NG+, multiplayer)
- Minor visual issues that don't break gameplay

### Release Notes
Create `docs/RELEASE-NOTES-1.0.md`:
```markdown
# Forgotten Depths II — Release 1.0.0

## Features
- 4-character party with 6 races and 6 classes
- 3 dungeons across 7 floors total
- Turn-based tactical combat with Vancian spellcasting
- Town hub with Temple, Shop, Tavern, Guild, Town Hall
- Quest system with NPCs and progression gates
- Save/Load with 5 slots
- Full audio (music + SFX) with volume controls

## Known Issues
- See KNOWN-ISSUES.md

## Credits
- AI Fleet Dev Team
- Game mechanics: D&D 5e SRD 5.1 (CC-BY 4.0)
```

### Tests
- `src/__tests__/integration/packaged-build.test.ts`:
  - Verify all required data files (races, classes, monsters, items, spells, maps, encounters, loot-tables, rumors, quests) are present in build output
  - Verify all audio assets present
  - Verify build artifacts (`out/main`, `out/preload`, `out/renderer`) exist
- `src/__tests__/integration/save-compatibility.test.ts`:
  - Save format version stamped on all saves
  - Loading a v1.0 save works in v1.0
  - Future: define migration policy for v1.1

### Final Verification Checklist
Create `docs/FINAL-VERIFICATION.md`:
```markdown
# Forgotten Depths II — Final Verification

- [ ] `npm install` clean
- [ ] `npm test` all pass
- [ ] `npm run build` succeeds
- [ ] `npm run package` produces installer
- [ ] Installer runs on Windows 10+
- [ ] Installer runs on macOS 12+
- [ ] Installer runs on Linux (Ubuntu 22.04)
- [ ] First launch shows main menu within 3 seconds
- [ ] Complete playthrough succeeds (all 3 quests)
- [ ] No console errors during play
- [ ] All audio plays correctly
- [ ] All visual transitions smooth
- [ ] Save/Load works across app restarts
- [ ] About screen shows version 1.0.0
- [ ] README, CONTROLS, RELEASE-NOTES present
```

## Done When
- `npm run build` produces a working packaged app
- Installer artifacts exist in `dist-electron/`
- Game is playable start to finish with no critical bugs
- All documentation present (README, CONTROLS, RELEASE-NOTES, KNOWN-ISSUES, FINAL-VERIFICATION)
- About screen displays v1.0.0
- All visual + audio polish in place
- Final verification checklist 100% checked
- `npm test` passes
- `npm run build` succeeds
- `npm run package` succeeds
